steps.title: Find the linear coefficient
steps.content:
From the graph, the vertex has $x$-coordinate $-1$. For $y=2x^2+bx+c$, the axis of symmetry is
$$x=-\frac{b}{2(2)}=-\frac{b}{4}$$
Set this equal to $-1$:
$$-\frac{b}{4}=-1$$
$$b=4$$

steps.title: Find the constant term
steps.content: At the $y$-intercept, $x=0$, so the equation gives $y=c$. The graph shows a $y$-intercept of $-6$, so $c=-6$.
